title: session — Eloquent observers
description: Two observers — SessionObserver (Session model) + SessionAttendanceObserver (attendance pivot). Together they enforce entitlement gating, scoping-tuple integrity, coach same-tenant invariants, capacity gates, and the safeguarding-flag write path.

handles: creating -> populate ULID prefix `ses_`.
creating -> refuse when tenant lacks `sports_sessions` entitlement (SESSION_FEATURE_NOT_ENTITLED, 402).
creating -> refuse when resource_booking.tenant_id != session.tenant_id (SESSION_RESOURCE_BOOKING_TENANT_MISMATCH, 422 — cross-tenant FK smuggling).
creating -> refuse when resource_booking.facility.branch_id != session.branch_id (SESSION_RESOURCE_BOOKING_BRANCH_MISMATCH, 422).
creating -> refuse when resource_booking.status IN ('cancelled', 'no_show', 'completed') (SESSION_RESOURCE_BOOKING_UNAVAILABLE, 409).
creating -> refuse when another live session already references this resource_booking_id (unique index would raise; observer surfaces the readable error SESSION_RESOURCE_BOOKING_UNAVAILABLE, 409).

creating -> refuse team_id / season_id / event_id / head_coach_id / assistant_coach_ids entries / supervising_staff_id with tenant_id != session.tenant_id (returns per-field TENANT_MISMATCH errors, 422 warn severity).
creating -> refuse when session_type IN ('private_lesson', 'group_lesson') AND tenant lacks session_private_lessons entitlement (SESSION_PRIVATE_LESSONS_NOT_ENTITLED, 402).
creating -> refuse when coach_rating_input IS NOT NULL AND tenant lacks session_coach_ratings entitlement (SESSION_COACH_RATINGS_NOT_ENTITLED, 402).
creating -> refuse when lesson_plan_url IS NOT NULL AND tenant lacks session_lesson_plans entitlement (SESSION_LESSON_PLANS_NOT_ENTITLED, 402).
creating -> refuse when curriculum_topic > 64 chars AND tenant lacks session_lesson_plans entitlement.
creating -> refuse when head_coach_id IS NOT NULL AND head_coach_id present in assistant_coach_ids (SESSION_COACH_SELF_ASSIST, 422).
creating -> refuse when assistant_coach_ids has > 3 entries (SESSION_TOO_MANY_ASSISTANTS, 422).
creating -> refuse when attendance_min > attendance_capacity (SESSION_INVALID_ATTENDANCE_RANGE, 422).
creating -> refuse when status IN ('in_progress', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'rescheduled') (SESSION_INITIAL_STATUS_INVALID, 422 — only scheduled + confirmed valid initial values).
creating -> consume 1 unit of `session_slot` entitlement against (tenant_id) for the ISO calendar week of resource_booking.starts_at. Refuses with SESSION_QUOTA_EXCEEDED (402).
creating -> derive slug from name via helper (team-slug + iso-week format for team sessions; type + athlete + date for standalone).
creating -> default status='scheduled', checked_in_athletes_count=0, absent_athletes_count=0, safeguarding_incident_flag=false, session_rating_count=0.
created -> invalidate caches `ses:list:{tenant}`, `ses:today:{tenant}`, `ses:coach:{head_coach_id}` when set.
created -> fire SessionScheduled. Fire SessionCoachAssigned per assigned coach.
saving (tenant_id changed) -> refuse SESSION_TENANT_IMMUTABLE.
saving (branch_id changed) -> refuse SESSION_BRANCH_IMMUTABLE.
saving (resource_booking_id changed via PATCH endpoint) -> refuse SESSION_BOOKING_IMMUTABLE_VIA_PATCH (must use reschedule endpoint).

saving (resource_booking_id changed via reschedule endpoint) -> verify same-tenant + same-branch on new booking; verify booking is in an attachable state; free the old booking (release its slot); fire SessionRescheduled with before/after snapshots.
saving (team_id changed post-confirmed) -> refuse SESSION_TEAM_IMMUTABLE_POST_CONFIRM.
saving (session_type changed post-scheduled) -> refuse SESSION_TYPE_IMMUTABLE_POST_SCHEDULED.

saving (head_coach_id changed post-scheduled) -> compute notice_hours_before_start; when < 24h, require caller carry sessions.assign_coach.short-notice permission (SESSION_SHORT_NOTICE_COACH_REASSIGN_REQUIRES_PERMISSION, 403). Fire SessionCoachReassigned with audit_severity='critical' when < 24h AND minors registered on the session.
saving (head_coach_id / assistant_coach_ids changed) -> verify same-tenant on the new values.
saving (curriculum_topic / lesson_plan_url changed) -> fire SessionCurriculumTopicUpdated. Refuse lesson_plan_url when tenant lacks session_lesson_plans.
saving (attendance_capacity / attendance_min changed) -> re-validate range (min <= capacity). Fire SessionAttendanceCapacityChanged.
saving (coach_rating_input changed) -> refuse when tenant lacks session_coach_ratings.

saving (safeguarding_incident_flag flipped true) -> require session_notes IS NOT NULL (SESSION_SAFEGUARDING_NOTES_REQUIRED, 422 warn). Fire SessionMinorSafeguardingIncident with regulator-facing severity when any registered athlete is a minor (checked via athlete_enrollment linkage). Cannot flip false via PATCH — safeguarding flags are permanent once set.

saving (status transitioning) -> validate transition against state machine (scheduled -> confirmed -> in_progress -> completed; scheduled|confirmed|in_progress -> cancelled; scheduled|confirmed -> rescheduled). Refuse invalid with SESSION_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID (422). Fire the matching specific event.

saving (status -> cancelled) -> refuse when cancellation_reason IS NULL (SESSION_CANCELLATION_REASON_REQUIRED, 422). Set cancellation_notice_at = now(). When notice_hours_before_start < 24, require sessions.cancel.short-notice permission. Fire SessionCancelled + trigger the cancellation broadcast.
saving (status -> in_progress) -> capture checked_in_count_at_start snapshot in the event payload.

saving (any other mutation) -> fire SessionCurriculumTopicUpdated / SessionAttendanceCapacityChanged / SessionUpdated per changed field (no umbrella SessionUpdated event since specific events cover every audit-worthy field).
updated -> invalidate `ses:full:{session}`, `ses:today:{tenant}`, `ses:coach:{head_coach_id}` caches.
deleting (soft-delete via archive path) -> require Session.status IN ('scheduled', 'cancelled', 'completed'). Refused otherwise.
deleting (hard-delete) -> refuse SESSION_PUBLIC_HARD_DELETE_FORBIDDEN (403) unless platform-support two-person CLI path.
forceDeleted -> fire SessionDeleted (mode='hard'). Cascade at DB layer (session_attendance CASCADE FK on session_id).
restored -> fire SessionRestored with days_archived.
creating -> populate ULID prefix `sat_`.
creating -> refuse when session.tenant_id != attendance.tenant_id.
creating -> refuse when athlete.tenant_id != attendance.tenant_id (SESSION_ATHLETE_TENANT_MISMATCH, 422 warn).
creating -> refuse when athlete_enrollment_id IS NOT NULL AND athlete_enrollment.athlete_id != attendance.athlete_id.
creating -> refuse when an active row already exists for the same (session_id, athlete_id) pair (SESSION_ATHLETE_ALREADY_REGISTERED, 409).
creating -> refuse when session.attendance_capacity IS NOT NULL AND current active row count >= session.attendance_capacity (SESSION_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED, 409).
creating -> refuse when athlete's age_group / gender fails session eligibility (SESSION_ATTENDANCE_ATHLETE_ELIGIBILITY_MISMATCH, 409).
creating -> default status='registered'.
created -> increment session.checked_in_athletes_count / absent_athletes_count via denormalisation update (fires only on status transitions, not creation).
saving (session_id OR athlete_id changed post-create) -> refuse (compositional identity — to reassign, delete + recreate).

saving (status transitioning) -> validate transition (registered -> checked_in|absent|late|excused; checked_in -> late is not permitted; excused is terminal). Refuse invalid with SESSION_ATTENDANCE_INVALID_STATE (409).

saving (status -> checked_in OR late) -> require session.status IN ('confirmed', 'in_progress') OR session.starts_at within [now - 24h, now + 24h] (grace window). Set checked_in_at = now(). Compute late_minutes when past session.starts_at + grace; auto-set status='late' when applicable. Fire SessionAthleteCheckedIn (or SessionAthleteMarkedLate).
saving (status -> absent) -> fire SessionAthleteMarkedAbsent.
saving (checked_in / late / absent status transition) -> update session.checked_in_athletes_count + absent_athletes_count denormalised counters.
deleting (soft-delete) -> refuse when session.status='completed' (soft-delete would rewrite history; the session already ended). Fire SessionAthleteRemoved.
deleted -> decrement session denormalised counters for the departed row's status.
forceDeleted -> DB-level only.
